title: Replace the Flat-Flex Cable (FFC) on the Tipper
summary: Remove the existing flat-flex cable and install a replacement FFC through the A-axis PCA, cable carrier, column, and controller PCA, then return the operator station to service.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the tipper flat-flex cable requires replacement and the source-supported replacement path includes the A-axis PCA, cable carrier, column, and controller PCA.
not_for: Do not use this procedure if cable routing, loop size, clamp placement, or connection orientation cannot be matched to the removed cable using source-backed observations.
Do not use this procedure to interchange left and right ClearLink controller hardware; the source notes the hardware is the same but not interchangeable because IP addresses are different.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, access panels and controller box, remove and install the FFC, restore hardware, and restart the operator station using the referenced procedures.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before beginning this replacement.
Do not remove the four M8 button-head screws when releasing the controller box; loosen them and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
Make sure no wires are pinched when rehanging the control box and feeding excess cable back into the stand.
Ensure the correct controller is installed; left and right ClearLink controller hardware is the same but not interchangeable because IP addresses are different.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the tipper and controller box
Access to the referenced LOTO and operator station restart procedures

access_required: Access to the station side panel if removal is necessary
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Replacement flat-flex cable is available before removal begins.
external_references: Referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before opening panels and handling internal cable connections.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before beginning the flat-flex cable replacement.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in a locked out and tagged out state and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied per the referenced procedure before any panel, clamp, or controller box work begins.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper is not locked out/tagged out or maintenance begins before LOTO is completed.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced LOTO procedure is not available.
The tipper cannot be placed in a locked out/tagged out state.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
The nearby procedure states that the tipper must be LOTO'd.

steps.title: Gather replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required materials and tools are available before disassembly starts.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.
steps.expected_result: All listed parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement FFC and all listed tools and PPE are present before hardware removal begins.
steps.failure_condition: Required replacement part, PPE, or tools are missing.

actions.label: Verify materials and tools
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.

stop_or_escalate_if: The replacement flat-flex cable is not available.
Required PPE or tools are not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the documented replacement part, PPE, and tools: replacement flat-flex cable, safety glasses, gloves, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, 4-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, tool to cut wire ties, and wire ties.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and internal components needed for the FFC replacement.
steps.instruction: If necessary, remove the nine self-tapping screws with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket and remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and the internal access area is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: The required access area is open and the panel is removed only if needed.
steps.failure_condition: The panel cannot be removed or access to the motor area is still obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the nine self-tapping screws with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket and remove the station side panel to access the motor, if necessary.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall flat-flex cable area and related clamp hardware after access is opened.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed without damage.
Required access to the motor or cable area is still not available after panel removal.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, remove the nine self-tapping screws with a 3/8-in. wrench or socket and remove the station side panel to access the motor.
The nearby procedure states that the station side panel may need to be removed.

steps.title: Replace the FFC at the A-axis PCA
steps.purpose: Disconnect the old cable and connect the replacement cable at the A-axis PCA end.
steps.instruction: At the A-axis PCA, remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps, unplug the old FFC, plug in the new FFC, and reinstall the FFC clamps with the M3 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The new FFC is connected at the A-axis PCA and the two FFC clamps are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: The new FFC is plugged into the A-axis PCA and both clamps are secured with the four M3 socket-head screws.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC cannot be unplugged or reconnected, the clamp hardware cannot be reinstalled, or connection orientation cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Remove A-axis PCA clamps
actions.exact_action: Remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps.

actions.label: Swap FFC at A-axis PCA
actions.exact_action: Unplug the old FFC and plug in the new FFC.

actions.label: Reinstall A-axis PCA clamps
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the FFC clamps with the M3 socket-head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: FFC clamps and M3 socket-head screw hardware used during flat-flex cable replacement.
Tip PCA / A-axis PCA connection area for the FFC.

stop_or_escalate_if: The connection orientation cannot be matched to the removed cable.
The clamps or M3 socket-head screws cannot be reinstalled correctly.

source_evidence.text: At the A-axis PCA, remove the four M3 socket-head screws holding the two FFC clamps, unplug the old FFC, plug in the new FFC, and reinstall the FFC clamps with the M3 socket-head screws.
At the A-axis PCA, the technician removes four M3 socket-head screws holding two FFC clamps, unplugs the old flat-flex cable, installs the new FFC, and reinstalls the clamps.

steps.title: Document the existing cable routing
steps.purpose: Preserve the original routing and loop reference before removing the old cable.
steps.instruction: Note how the old FFC routes into the cable carrier and take a picture before removal.
steps.expected_result: A visual reference of the original routing is captured for comparison during installation.
steps.healthy_condition: The original routing into the cable carrier is documented before the old FFC is removed.
steps.failure_condition: The original routing is not documented before removal.

actions.label: Record existing routing
actions.exact_action: Note how the old FFC routes into the cable carrier and take a picture before removal.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Routing path reference to compare with the existing cable path into the cable carrier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The original routing cannot be observed clearly enough to document.
The cable path cannot be matched later to the removed condition.

source_evidence.text: Note how the old FFC routes into the cable carrier and take a picture before removal.

steps.title: Remove carrier-end clamp hardware and wire ties
steps.purpose: Free the old FFC from the cable carrier entry point while preserving clamp orientation details.
steps.instruction: Remove the M3 socket-head screws holding the FFC clamps, cut the one wire tie at both ends of the cable carrier, and remove the FFC clamps from the FFC while noting how the FFC feeds through the clamp.
steps.expected_result: The old FFC is released from the cable carrier entry hardware and the clamp orientation is noted.
steps.healthy_condition: The clamp hardware is removed without losing the observed feed orientation through the clamp.
steps.failure_condition: Clamp orientation is not noted, hardware cannot be removed, or the cable carrier ends cannot be freed.

actions.label: Remove carrier clamp screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the M3 socket-head screws holding the FFC clamps.

actions.label: Cut wire ties
actions.exact_action: Cut the one wire tie at both ends of the cable carrier.

actions.label: Note clamp feed orientation
actions.exact_action: Remove the FFC clamps from the FFC while noting how the FFC feeds through the clamp.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp hardware associated with the flat-flex cable.
Cable routing and entry area reference for the cable carrier.

stop_or_escalate_if: Clamp position or feed orientation through the clamp cannot be determined.
The cable carrier end hardware cannot be removed cleanly.

source_evidence.text: Remove the M3 socket-head screws holding the FFC clamps, cut the one wire tie at both ends of the cable carrier, and remove the FFC clamps from the FFC while noting how the FFC feeds through the clamp.

steps.title: Remove the old FFC from the cable carrier
steps.purpose: Clear the old cable from the carrier path so the replacement cable can be installed.
steps.instruction: Pull the old FFC through and out of the cable carrier.
steps.expected_result: The old FFC is fully removed from the cable carrier.
steps.healthy_condition: The old FFC is removed completely from the cable carrier path.
steps.failure_condition: The old FFC cannot be pulled through the carrier or becomes obstructed.

actions.label: Remove old FFC from carrier
actions.exact_action: Pull the old FFC through and out of the cable carrier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The old FFC cannot be removed from the cable carrier.
Unexpected obstruction or damage is encountered in the cable carrier.

source_evidence.text: Pull the old FFC through and out of the cable carrier.

steps.title: Feed the new FFC through the cable carrier
steps.purpose: Install the replacement cable along the original carrier path.
steps.instruction: Feed the new FFC through the cable carrier, paying attention to how the FFC loops as it enters the cable carrier.
steps.expected_result: The new FFC is routed through the cable carrier with loop behavior matching the removed cable as closely as possible.
steps.healthy_condition: The new FFC passes through the cable carrier and the entry loop resembles the original observed condition.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC cannot be fed through the carrier or the loop cannot be matched to the removed condition.

actions.label: Route new FFC through carrier
actions.exact_action: Feed the new FFC through the cable carrier, paying attention to how the FFC loops as it enters the cable carrier.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Routing path and loop behavior reference for the cable entering the carrier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The loop size or routing cannot be matched to the removed cable.
The cable does not feed cleanly through the carrier.

source_evidence.text: Feed the new FFC through the cable carrier, paying attention to how the FFC loops as it enters the cable carrier.

steps.title: Install and position the carrier-entry clamp
steps.purpose: Secure the new FFC at the cable carrier entry with the correct clamp orientation and loop size.
steps.instruction: Install the FFC clamp onto the FFC as it enters the cable carrier, making sure the small leg is under the FFC; adjust the clamp placement until the loop is a similar size as when removed, then mount it with the two M3 socket-head screws.
steps.expected_result: The clamp is installed with the small leg under the FFC and the loop size is similar to the removed condition.
steps.healthy_condition: Clamp orientation matches the documented condition and the loop size is similar to the original.
steps.failure_condition: The small leg is not under the FFC, the loop size cannot be matched, or the clamp cannot be mounted securely.

actions.label: Install carrier-entry clamp
actions.exact_action: Install the FFC clamp onto the FFC as it enters the cable carrier, making sure the small leg is under the FFC.

actions.label: Match loop size
actions.exact_action: Adjust the clamp placement until the loop is a similar size as when removed.

actions.label: Secure clamp
actions.exact_action: Mount it with the two M3 socket-head screws.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp hardware associated with the flat-flex cable.

stop_or_escalate_if: The clamp orientation cannot be confirmed.
The loop size cannot be adjusted to a similar size as when removed.

source_evidence.text: Install the FFC clamp onto the FFC as it enters the cable carrier, making sure the small leg is under the FFC; adjust the clamp placement until the loop is a similar size as when removed, then mount it with the two M3 socket-head screws.

steps.title: Secure the air line to the clamp
steps.purpose: Restore the retained relationship between the air line and the FFC clamp.
steps.instruction: Use a wire tie to secure the air line to the FFC clamp as before.
steps.expected_result: The air line is secured to the FFC clamp with a wire tie in the same manner as before removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The air line is tied to the clamp as previously configured.
steps.failure_condition: The air line cannot be secured as before or the prior configuration cannot be matched.

actions.label: Tie air line to clamp
actions.exact_action: Use a wire tie to secure the air line to the FFC clamp as before.

stop_or_escalate_if: The previous air line tie position cannot be matched.

source_evidence.text: Use a wire tie to secure the air line to the FFC clamp as before.

steps.title: Release the controller box
steps.purpose: Gain access to the controller PCA connection without fully removing the controller box fasteners.
steps.instruction: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners, and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is released and lifted off the loosened fasteners for access.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller box is free for access while the fasteners remain in place.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box cannot be released, or the fasteners are removed contrary to the source instruction.

actions.label: Loosen controller box screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners.

actions.label: Lift controller box free
actions.exact_action: Lift the box off the loosened fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box location and the four M8 button-head screws used to release it.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller box cannot be released from the loosened fasteners.
The box cannot be moved without risking wire damage.

source_evidence.text: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners, and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.

steps.title: Disconnect the old FFC from the controller PCA and remove it from the column
steps.purpose: Free the old cable from the controller end and remove it from the column path.
steps.instruction: Unplug the FFC from the controller PCA and pull the FFC down through and out of the column.
steps.expected_result: The old FFC is disconnected from the controller PCA and removed from the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The old FFC is fully removed from the controller PCA and column path.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC cannot be unplugged from the controller PCA or cannot be removed from the column.

actions.label: Disconnect controller-end FFC

steps.instruction: Feed the new FFC into and down through the column until it comes out of the upper grommet at the control box.
steps.expected_result: The new FFC is routed through the column and exits at the upper grommet at the control box.
steps.healthy_condition: The new FFC comes out of the upper grommet at the control box without obstruction.
steps.failure_condition: The FFC does not emerge at the upper grommet or cannot be routed through the column.

actions.label: Route FFC through column

steps.purpose: Restore the controller-end electrical connection for the replacement cable.
steps.instruction: Plug the FFC into the controller PCA at the documented FFC connection.
steps.expected_result: The new FFC is connected to the controller PCA.
steps.healthy_condition: The FFC is plugged into the documented controller PCA connection.
steps.failure_condition: The connection point cannot be identified or the FFC cannot be connected correctly.

steps.title: Rehang the control box and check for pinched wires
steps.purpose: Restore the controller box to its mounting position while protecting the cable and other wiring.
steps.instruction: Hang the control box on the M8 button-head screws on the stand, feed excess cable back into the stand, and make sure no wires are pinched.
steps.expected_result: The control box is rehung on the stand and excess cable is managed without pinching wires.
steps.healthy_condition: The control box is seated on the M8 button-head screws and no wires are pinched.
steps.failure_condition: The control box cannot be repositioned without pinching wires or excess cable cannot be fed back safely.

actions.label: Manage excess cable
actions.exact_action: Feed excess cable back into the stand.

actions.label: Check for pinched wires
actions.exact_action: Make sure no wires are pinched.

Controller box mounting position on the stand.

steps.expected_result: The controller box is secured to the stand.
steps.healthy_condition: All four M8 button-head screws are tightened and the controller box is secure.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box cannot be secured or the fasteners cannot be tightened properly.

steps.title: Reinstall any removed side panels
steps.purpose: Restore guarding and enclosure panels after the cable replacement is complete.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.
steps.expected_result: Any removed side panels are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: All removed panels are back in place.
steps.failure_condition: Panels cannot be reinstalled or guarding remains incomplete.

actions.label: Reinstall panels
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: A removed panel cannot be reinstalled.
Guarding cannot be restored to its prior condition.

source_evidence.text: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service after the replacement is complete.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station returns to service after the FFC replacement.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The referenced restart procedure is not available.
The operator station does not return to service after restart.

source_evidence.text: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure.

success_criteria: The replacement FFC is installed and connected at the A-axis PCA and controller PCA.
The FFC is routed through the cable carrier and column with similar loop and clamp placement as before removal.
The controller box is reinstalled and secured to the stand.
healthy_conditions: The new FFC is connected at both the A-axis PCA and controller PCA.
Clamp placement and loop size are similar to the removed cable condition.
The small leg of the clamp is under the FFC at the cable carrier entry.
The air line is secured to the FFC clamp as before.
No wires are pinched when the control box is rehung and tightened.
failure_conditions: Cable routing, loop size, clamp placement, or connection orientation cannot be matched to the removed cable using source-backed observations.
The controller box cannot be repositioned without pinching wires.
The FFC cannot be connected correctly at the A-axis PCA or controller PCA.
The cable cannot be routed through the cable carrier or column.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the cable routing, loop size, clamp placement, or connection orientation cannot be matched to the removed cable using source-backed observations.
Stop and escalate if the controller box cannot be repositioned without pinching wires.
Stop and escalate if the tipper cannot be locked out/tagged out before work begins.
Verify the correct controller is installed; left and right ClearLink controller hardware is the same but not interchangeable because IP addresses are different.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide the full OCR text for pages 161-164, so step-level quoted text beyond the candidate and artifact summaries is limited.
No source-supported estimated time for the FFC replacement procedure is present in the packet.
No torque values are provided in the packet for the M3 or M8 fasteners.
No explicit post-restart functional verification steps are provided in the packet.
No command-line or HMI commands are provided in the packet.
